title: Decoding haptic and imagined stimulus size in the human cortex.

assets.abstract: Human neuroimaging studies indicate that the early visual cortex (EVC), including the primary visual cortex (V1), is involved in haptic exploration of objects, even when visual information is not available. However, it remains unknown whether the features of haptically explored objects, like size, are represented in the EVC. Here, we investigated whether we can use the activity patterns in the EVC and other task-relevant brain regions to decode stimulus size during haptic exploration, and whethe

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Mental imagery — visual imagery as real perception, imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.
